scope: Battle Command tactical map selection mode

checks.name: npm run build
checks.notes: TypeScript project build and Vite production build completed successfully.

checks.name: desktop browser QA
checks.notes: In-app browser verified Theater -> Army Camp -> Deployment -> Battle Command at http://127.0.0.1:5173/.

qaFlow: Opened the local app and moved through Theater -> Army Camp -> Deployment -> Battle Command.
Confirmed the selected-unit command panel includes `選択モード` and `.map-selection-inspector`.
Pressed `選択モード`; the battle map field became `command-select`, five frontline segments and one facility were visible, and the command status read `選択確認`.
Clicked the visible structure. The selection inspector changed from `担当施設` to `確認施設` without issuing a facility assignment.
Ran battle at 3x until enemies spawned, clicked an enemy group, and confirmed the enemy intent panel opened while focused-target count remained 0 and inspected enemy count became 1.
Clicked another player unit and confirmed exactly one selected player unit, selected-unit panel update, selected frontline update, and map inspector update.
implementedBehavior: `MapCommandMode` now includes `select` as a visible selection/inspection mode separate from command issuing.
Selection mode automatically shows frontline and facility layers to make clickable tactical entities discoverable.
The command compass includes a `選択` tile and the selected-unit button row includes `選択モード`.
`map-selection-inspector` summarizes the selected brigade, inspected enemy, selected frontline, and inspected/assigned facility.
Clicking a structure in selection mode inspects it without assigning it to the selected unit.
Clicking an enemy in selection mode inspects it without setting the selected unit's focus target.
Clicking a player unit in selection mode selects it, clears stale enemy/facility inspection state, and follows its assigned frontline.
outOfScope: No BattleState or save schema fields were added.
No mobile/cellphone QA was run because it remains outside the current active target.
This pass does not add box selection or multi-select command groups.
